name: Raycast Dungeon — can an LLM escape a 3D maze?
description: A first-person maze-escape benchmark. The agent perceives the maze only egocentrically (open/wall ahead-left-right, corridor depths, a compass bearing to the exit) and issues one discrete move per turn. It must reach the exit before the step cap. LLMs are poor at building a spatial map from first-person text, so they loop, backtrack, and walk into walls.

binding_vow.description: First-person grid maze. Each turn: a JSON observation (ahead/left/right/behind = open|wall, corridor depths, 8-way exit bearing, exit distance, facing, steps_left, last_action_bumped). Action: one of forward/turn_left/turn_right/turn_around. forward into a wall is a no-op bump. Win by reaching the exit cell within the step cap.

observation_space.description: { ahead, left, right, behind: 'open'|'wall'; dist_ahead, dist_left, dist_right: int; facing: 'N'|'E'|'S'|'W'; exit_bearing: 8-way compass; exit_distance: int; inventory; on_item; steps_left: int; last_action_bumped: 'true'|'false' }

action_space.description: forward = advance one cell in facing (bump if wall); turn_left/right = rotate 90°; turn_around = rotate 180°

reward.description: -0.02 per step; extra -0.05 on a wall bump; +1.0 terminal for reaching the exit. Positive iff escaped, negative iff floundering.
